speaker: patient
text: Hi, Doctor. I’ve been feeling really unwell the past couple of days.

speaker: doctor
text: Hello — thanks for coming in. Can you tell me more about what’s been going on?

speaker: patient
text: I’ve had a high fever, chills, and my heart has been racing — especially when I’m lying down.

speaker: doctor
text: Any other symptoms? Cough, shortness of breath, chest discomfort?

speaker: doctor
text: Based on your history and today’s assessment, your primary diagnosis is Essential Hypertension, confirmed as of September 5th.

speaker: doctor
text: I’m prescribing Metformin 500mg twice daily, starting today — this will help manage your blood sugar, which we’ll monitor closely.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic blood pressure today is 77 mmHg — within acceptable range for now, though we’ll keep an eye on it.

speaker: patient
text: Yes — the palpitations are what worry me most. They started two days ago and haven’t let up.

speaker: doctor
text: Your fasting blood glucose came back at 7.4 mmol/L — slightly elevated, which supports initiating Metformin.

speaker: doctor
text: Your temperature is quite high — 39.6 °C — suggesting an acute febrile illness. We’ll need to investigate possible infection.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve been taking acetaminophen, but the fever keeps coming back. My throat feels sore too.

speaker: doctor
text: Let’s check your throat and listen to your lungs. Given your hypertension and current symptoms, we’ll also reassess cardiovascular stability.

speaker: doctor
text: I just rechecked your temperature — it’s now 38.7 °C, so there’s some response to antipyretics, but it’s still concerning.

speaker: patient
text: My legs have felt a bit swollen since yesterday — and I’m more tired than usual.

speaker: doctor
text: Given your persistent hypertension and new signs of possible volume overload or early heart strain, I’m going to switch you from no antihypertensive to Lisinopril 10mg once daily, starting today.

speaker: patient
text: Okay — should I stop the Metformin? And is the Lisinopril safe with the fever?

speaker: doctor
text: No, continue Metformin as prescribed. Lisinopril is appropriate here, but we’ll monitor renal function and potassium closely given the fever and potential dehydration.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve also noticed dizziness when standing up quickly — maybe that’s related?

speaker: doctor
text: That could reflect orthostatic changes — let’s check your BP again while seated and standing. Also, how’s your fluid intake?

speaker: doctor
text: Your seated diastolic pressure is now 72 mmHg — a slight drop from the earlier reading of 77. Let’s hold off on further antihypertensive escalation until we see how you respond to Lisinopril and address the fever.
